title: Introduction To The Garden of Bright Waters - One Hundred and Twenty Asiatic Love Poems
content:
Head in hand, I look at the paper leaf;
    It is still white.

    I look at the ink
    Dry on the end of my brush.

    My soul sleeps.
    Will it ever wake?

    I walk a little in the pouring of the sun
    And pass my hands over the higher flowers.

    There is the soft green forest,
    There are the sweet lines of the mountains
    Carved with snow, red in the sunlight.

    I see the slow march of the clouds,
    I hear the crows jeering, and I come back

    To sit and look at the paper leaf,
    Which is still white
    Under my brush.

    From the Chinese of Chang-Chi (770-850).
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Powys Mathers (As Translator), titled "Introduction To The Garden of Bright Waters - One Hundred and Twenty Asiatic Love Poems", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
